title: Advanced Practice with Mausuf and Sifah in the Qur'an
summary: This session focuses on deepening understanding of mausuf (modified noun) and sifah (adjective) through examples from the Quran. The teacher emphasizes applying four criteria: gender, number, final harakah, and definiteness/indefiniteness. Students are encouraged to identify these in Quranic verses, with ample examples provided.
key_points: Mausuf and sifah must match in gender, number, final harakah (fatha, kasra, damma), and both be either definite (al-) or indefinite (tanwin).
Examples from the Qur'an include pairs like ajr kareem (noble reward) and ayat bayinat (clear signs), which fit all four criteria.
Proper nouns are exceptions as they remain definite despite tanween, requiring careful identification.
Students must systematically test each pair against the four rules to confirm mausuf-sifah relationship, ensuring translation accuracy.
